Title: Security Verification Form — Rendered for Unauthenticated Visitor
Preconditions:
[PRE:CLEAR_SESSION]
No active storefront session. Target userId known via @td(IMPERSONATE_TARGET.userId).
Steps:
[PRE:SIGNOUT]
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/account/impersonate/@td(IMPERSONATE_TARGET.userId)
[WAIT] Page fully renders
[ASSERT] URL remains on /account/impersonate/{userId} — no redirect to /sign-in
Assertions:
[DOM] h1 text = 'Security verification'
[DOM] Subtitle paragraph 'Confirm your identity to continue' visible
[DOM] Description paragraph 'You are logging in on behalf of a customer. The session will be audited.' visible
[DOM] input[type='email'] present and enabled
[DOM] input[type='password'] present and enabled
[DOM] 'Verify and continue' submit button visible
[DOM] 'Cancel' button visible
[NAV] URL remains /account/impersonate/{userId} — no redirect occurred
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] GET /account/impersonate/{userId} returns HTTP 200
[CONSOLE] No JS errors on page load
Failure_Signals: Page redirects to /sign-in instead of rendering form; h1 text missing or different; subtitle or description absent; 'Verify and continue' button absent; 404 on route; console TypeError
Cleanup: [NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}} to clear page state
References: VCST-4905, VCST-4725, BL-AUTH-005; Executed PASS 2026-05-11 via qa-frontend-expert (evidence: reports/test-lifecycle/TLC-2026-05-11-2114/execution-evidence/auth-069-PASS-anonymous-form-rendered.png)

Title: Form Flow — Valid Operator Credentials Impersonate Target
Preconditions:
[PRE:CLEAR_SESSION]
Unauthenticated user. SUPPORT_AGENT (USR-001) is assigned a role that includes StorefrontPermissions.CanImpersonate (configured in Admin SPA > Security > Roles > [role] > Permissions). IMPERSONATE_TARGET is an active customer in a different org. State: unauthenticated → form flow → impersonated session.
Steps:
[PRE:SIGNOUT]
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/account/impersonate/@td(IMPERSONATE_TARGET.userId)
[WAIT] ImpersonateForm renders
[ASSERT] Verify 'Verify and continue' button is disabled (both fields empty, meta.valid=false)
[ACT] Fill email input: @td(SUPPORT_AGENT.email)
[WAIT] Email field populated
[ACT] Fill password input: @td(SUPPORT_AGENT.password)
[WAIT] Password field populated
[ASSERT] Verify 'Verify and continue' button becomes enabled (meta.valid=true)
[ACT] Click 'Verify and continue' button
[WAIT] Step transitions through verify → impersonate phases (loading state visible)
[ASSERT] Green VcAlert (success color) appears with text 'Verification successful. Switching to customer view…'
[WAIT] Wait up to 3s for broadcast-triggered tab reload
[ASSERT] After reload: storefront header reflects target identity (@td(IMPERSONATE_TARGET.name))
[ASSERT] Impersonation banner is visible on the resulting page
Assertions:
[STATE] Steps transition: idle → verify (loading) → impersonate → success
[DOM] Green success VcAlert shown: 'Verification successful. Switching to customer view…'
[STATE] After broadcast reload: session is TARGET user's session (@td(IMPERSONATE_TARGET.name) — not SUPPORT_AGENT)
[STATE] Impersonation banner visible after reload: operator name + 'logged in as' + target name
[NAV] URL redirects away from /account/impersonate/{userId} after reload
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] POST /connect/token (verify): returns 200 with access_token for SUPPORT_AGENT @td(SUPPORT_AGENT.email)
[API] POST /connect/token grant_type=impersonate; scope=offline_access; user_id=@td(IMPERSONATE_TARGET.userId) returns HTTP 200 with access_token, refresh_token, expires_in, token_type
[NETWORK] No 4xx/5xx on either /connect/token call
[CONSOLE] No JS errors during transition
Failure_Signals: Success VcAlert not shown; page does not reload after 3s; storefront session still shows SUPPORT_AGENT identity; impersonation banner absent; token exchange returns 4xx; form submit has no effect; console TypeError
References: VCST-4905, VCST-4725, BL-AUTH-005, BL-AUTH-006; Executed PASS 2026-05-11 v2 — both /connect/token calls returned 200, storefront switched into David's session, header shows 'John Mitchell logged in as David Kim' (evidence: reports/test-lifecycle/TLC-2026-05-11-2114/execution-evidence/auth-070-v2-john-david-PASS-impersonation-success.png)

Title: Silent Flow — Authenticated Operator (Role Includes CanImpersonate) Skips Verification Form
Preconditions:
[PRE:CLEAR_SESSION]
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:SUPPORT_AGENT]
SUPPORT_AGENT has StorefrontPermissions.CanImpersonate. IMPERSONATE_TARGET is an active customer in a different org (ORG-002). VERIFIED 2026-05-11 — USR-001 John Mitchell satisfies both conditions on vcst-qa; navigation to /account/impersonate/{David's userId} silently switches into David's session. State: authenticated as SUPPORT_AGENT → silent impersonation → target session.
Steps:
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/sign-in
[ACT] Fill 'Email': @td(SUPPORT_AGENT.email)
[WAIT] Email field populated
[ACT] Fill 'Password': @td(SUPPORT_AGENT.password)
[WAIT] Password field populated
[ACT] Click 'Sign In'
[WAIT] Redirect to account area
[ASSERT] Verify user is authenticated (header shows support user identity)
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/account/impersonate/@td(IMPERSONATE_TARGET.userId)
[WAIT] Silent flow activates
[ASSERT] Verify ImpersonateForm is NOT rendered (no email input; no password input; no 'Verify and continue' button)
[ASSERT] Verify VcLoaderOverlay shown with text 'Switching to customer view…'
[WAIT] Wait up to 3s for broadcast-triggered tab reload
[ASSERT] After reload: storefront header reflects target customer identity
[ASSERT] Impersonation banner is visible
Assertions:
[DOM] ImpersonateForm NOT rendered (form fields absent — silent flow bypasses form)
[DOM] VcLoaderOverlay shown with text 'Switching to customer view…'
[STATE] impersonateAuthenticated(userId) called automatically on mount — no credentials required
[STATE] After broadcast reload: session is target customer's session
[STATE] Impersonation banner visible: operator name + 'logged in as' + target name
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] POST /connect/token with grant_type=impersonate returns HTTP 200
[NETWORK] No separate credential authorization call before impersonate grant (silent path skips verify step)
[CONSOLE] No JS errors during silent redirect
Failure_Signals: ImpersonateForm rendered instead of silent flow (CanImpersonate not detected); VcLoaderOverlay not shown; page does not reload after 3s; banner absent; session cleared entirely; console TypeError
Cleanup: Stop impersonation via banner button and [PRE:CLEAR_SESSION]
References: VCST-4905, VCST-4725, BL-AUTH-005, BL-AUTH-006; Executed PASS 2026-05-11 v2 — John signed in then navigated to /account/impersonate/{David's userId}; form NOT rendered, single /connect/token impersonate call → 200, storefront silently switched into David's session (evidence: reports/test-lifecycle/TLC-2026-05-11-2114/execution-evidence/auth-077-v2-john-canimpersonate-PASS-silent-flow.png)

Title: Revert Impersonation — 'Revert back to own account' Restores Operator Session and Invalidates Target Token
Preconditions:
[PRE:CLEAR_SESSION]
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:SUPPORT_AGENT]
Impersonation of IMPERSONATE_TARGET is active — banner visible. PR #2280 introduces 'Revert back to own account' UI hook in top-header (top-header.vue) and mobile-menu (main-menu.vue) replacing the old account-menu 'Stop impersonation' option. AC#6: Previous (impersonation) token MUST be invalidated after revert. State: impersonated → revert → operator session restored + previous token invalid.
Steps:
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/sign-in
[ACT] Fill 'Email': @td(SUPPORT_AGENT.email)
[WAIT] Email field populated
[ACT] Fill 'Password': @td(SUPPORT_AGENT.password)
[WAIT] Password field populated
[ACT] Click 'Sign In'
[WAIT] Authenticated session established (header shows operator identity @td(SUPPORT_AGENT.name))
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/account/impersonate/@td(IMPERSONATE_TARGET.userId)
[WAIT] Silent flow completes — impersonation banner visible; session is target user's session
[ASSERT] Verify impersonation is active: header account menu button shows target user name (not @td(SUPPORT_AGENT.name))
[ACT] Observe header area — locate 'Revert back to own account' link/button in the desktop top-header (added by PR #2280 top-header.vue)
[ASSERT] 'Revert back to own account' item is visible in header during active impersonation
[ACT] Capture the current impersonation access_token value from localStorage or cookies (via browser DevTools) as {{IMPERSONATION_TOKEN}} for later invalidation check
[ACT] Click 'Revert back to own account' in the header
[WAIT] VcLoaderOverlay with revert loading text appears (slot content via vc-loader-overlay.vue PR #2280) then dismisses
[WAIT] Session transitions — broadcast to TabsType.OTHERS triggers other tabs; current tab redirects
Assertions:
[DOM] 'Revert back to own account' item visible in top-header during active impersonation (PR #2280 top-header.vue hook)
[STATE] After revert: impersonation banner disappears
[STATE] Account menu button reverts to operator's name (@td(SUPPORT_AGENT.name))
[NAV] URL redirects to {{FRONT_URL}}/ or {{FRONT_URL}}/account/dashboard — NOT /sign-in (operator session preserved, not signed out)
[STATE] Operator session remains active — header confirms @td(SUPPORT_AGENT.name) identity
[STATE] Previous impersonation token {{IMPERSONATION_TOKEN}} is invalidated — a subsequent API call using that token returns 401 (AC#6 — previous token MUST be invalidated)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] POST to revert/token endpoint (revertImpersonate from useImpersonate.ts) returns HTTP 200
[NETWORK] Follow-up GET {{FRONT_URL}}/api/... or POST /connect/token using {{IMPERSONATION_TOKEN}} returns HTTP 401 (token invalidated — AC#6 verified)
[CONSOLE] No JS errors during session restore
Failure_Signals: 'Revert back to own account' item not visible in header during impersonation (PR #2280 not deployed or revert hook missing); clicking revert results in full sign-out (operator session cleared); operator session does not restore — anonymous after exit; banner persists after exit; old impersonation token still returns 200 after revert (AC#6 violation); console TypeError
References: VCST-4906, VCST-4905, VCST-4725, BL-AUTH-006, BL-AUTH-007, BL-AUTH-011, ECL-14.1; Synced: VCST-4906 / PR #2280 (2026-05-14) — extended from old 'Stop impersonation via account menu' to 'Revert back to own account' via header hook (top-header.vue + main-menu.vue) + AC#6 previous-token-invalidation cross-layer check

Title: Nested Impersonation — Target Switch Within an Active Operator Session (by design)
Preconditions:
[PRE:CLEAR_SESSION]
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:SUPPORT_AGENT]
Impersonation of IMPERSONATE_TARGET active. IMPERSONATE_TARGET's role does NOT include CanImpersonate (permissions live on Roles). UI gate (PR #2280): the /company/members 'Login on behalf' row action is gated by CanImpersonate, so the impersonated session (which lacks it) should NOT surface that action. URL-based target switch: by design, switching the impersonation target from within an active operator session is permitted — the operator was authorized at session establishment (VCST-5174 closed Not a bug). This test covers the UI gate AND documents the by-design URL behavior.
Steps:
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/sign-in
[ACT] Fill 'Email': @td(SUPPORT_AGENT.email)
[WAIT] Email field populated
[ACT] Fill 'Password': @td(SUPPORT_AGENT.password)
[WAIT] Password field populated
[ACT] Click 'Sign In'
[WAIT] Authenticated session established
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/account/impersonate/@td(IMPERSONATE_TARGET.userId)
[WAIT] Impersonation active (banner visible)
--- UI ENTRY POINT CHECK ---
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/company/members
[WAIT] Company members table visible (target's org members)
[ACT] Click the row-level actions button for any non-self member row
[WAIT] Dropdown menu opens
[ASSERT] 'Login on behalf' option is NOT present in the dropdown (IMPERSONATE_TARGET lacks CanImpersonate — PR #2280 gate)
--- URL-BASED NESTING CHECK ---
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/account/impersonate/@td(SUPPORT_AGENT.userId)
[WAIT] Response observed
Assertions:
[DOM] Actions dropdown at /company/members does NOT show 'Login on behalf' for the impersonated session (target lacks CanImpersonate — PR #2280 members-dropdown-menu.vue UI gate; unchanged)
[DOM] URL-based target switch: by design the grant succeeds (HTTP 200) — operator session was authorized at establishment (hop 1); switching target within an active operator session does not re-prompt
[STATE] Impersonation target switches to the requested user; vc_operator_user_id remains the original operator
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] POST /connect/token (impersonate grant) returns HTTP 200 — target switch permitted by design
[CONSOLE] No unhandled exceptions
Failure_Signals: UI gate regression: 'Login on behalf' visible in /company/members dropdown for a session whose user lacks CanImpersonate; session loops; console TypeError
References: VCST-4906, VCST-4905, VCST-4725, VCST-5174 (closed Not a bug / by design 2026-06-01); BL-AUTH-009 (nested forbidden) contradicts by-design — flag for review; UI-gate portion still per PR #2280

Title: Admin SPA — platform:security:loginOnBehalf Permission Exists in Permission Catalog with Correct Label
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:ADMIN] (Admin SPA — back_url={{BACK_URL}})
Admin authenticated. **Important:** the 'Select permissions to assign' picker on existing roles HIDES permissions already assigned to that role — so opening a role that already owns platform:security:loginOnBehalf (e.g. 'Organization maintainer' on vcst-qa) will yield a false-negative on this test. Use the New-role inline permission tree (no prior assignments) as the canonical source-of-truth for this permission-catalog check.
Steps:
[NAV] {{BACK_URL}}/#!/workspace/security
[BLADE] Open 'Roles' list blade
[ACT] Click '+ Add' in the Roles toolbar to open a New role blade (inline permission tree shows the FULL permission catalog regardless of prior assignment — picker filtering does not apply)
[WAIT] New role detail blade renders with Name/Description inputs AND an inline permission tree below
[ACT] In the inline permission tree, scan for platform:security:loginOnBehalf — expand every collapsed category by clicking each chevron AND scroll the tree to its bottom (the permission lives under the 'Platform' category on vcst-qa as of 2026-05-12; do NOT stop scanning until either the row is found OR all categories are expanded and the scroll has reached the tree's end)
[ASSERT] A permission row with key 'platform:security:loginOnBehalf' is visible after the full scan
[ASSERT] Row label/description matches 'Login on behalf of a customer' (verbatim — no 'CanImpersonate' / 'StorefrontPermissions.CanImpersonate' legacy strings)
[ASSERT] Row exposes a selectable control — on the New-role inline tree this is `input[type=checkbox][ng-model='data.isChecked']` inside `label.list-item`
Assertions:
[DOM] Permission key 'platform:security:loginOnBehalf' present in the inline permission tree after full traversal
[DOM] Description text matches 'Login on behalf of a customer' verbatim
[DOM] Permission row exposes a selectable checkbox control (`input[type=checkbox]` in inline tree)
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] No JS errors on inline tree expand

Title: RBAC Negative — Operator Without loginOnBehalf: Form Flow impersonate Grant Rejected
Preconditions:
[PRE:CLEAR_SESSION]
Unauthenticated user. IMPERSONATE_TARGET (USR-007 David Kim) is assigned a viewer-tier role WITHOUT platform:security:loginOnBehalf per BL-AUTH-005/006 RBAC model (permissions live on roles, not users). OTHER_ORG_USER (USR-008 Carlos Rodriguez, ORG-003 BuildRight) is the impersonation target. State: form flow with credentials that authenticate but cannot pass the impersonate grant.
Steps:
[PRE:SIGNOUT]
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/account/impersonate/@td(OTHER_ORG_USER.userId)
[WAIT] ImpersonateForm rendered
[ACT] Fill email input: @td(IMPERSONATE_TARGET.email)
[WAIT] Email field populated
[ACT] Fill password input: @td(IMPERSONATE_TARGET.password)
[WAIT] Password field populated
[ACT] Click 'Verify and continue' submit button
[WAIT] Submit button shows loading spinner / disabled state (verify step in flight)
[WAIT] One of two observable rejection outcomes appears within 10s: (a) red VcAlert variant=danger visible in form area on /account/impersonate/{userId}; OR (b) hard route-level redirect to /403 Access denied page (h1 '403', h2 'Access denied', paragraph 'You do not have permissions to access the requested page', 'Home page' link visible) — vcst-qa storefront uses path (b) as of 2026-05-12
[ASSERT] Whichever branch fires: the rejection reason indicates failed impersonation/permission denied — NOT a credential-validation failure (NOT 'login_failed' / 'Check your credentials'). Branch (a) check: VcAlert text matches 'impersonate_failed' OR contains 'switching to the customer account failed' OR contains 'permission denied' / 'not authorized'. Branch (b) check: page is the global /403 Access denied template (h1='403', h2='Access denied')
[ASSERT] In BOTH branches: no impersonation banner mounts; URL is either /account/impersonate/{userId} (branch a) OR /403 (branch b) — never /home or /{target's account area}
Assertions:
[DOM] Either Branch (a): red VcAlert (variant=danger) visible in form area with impersonate-failed text — OR — Branch (b): /403 Access denied template (h1='403', h2='Access denied') with no inline form alert (form is unmounted)
[NAV] URL is either /account/impersonate/{userId} (branch a) OR /403 (branch b) — NOT redirected to home, NOT redirected into a target-user session
[STATE] Impersonation banner is NOT rendered in either branch
[STATE] No storefront session for OTHER_ORG_USER is established — localStorage auth token's JWT 'sub' claim remains the operator's userId (NOT @td(OTHER_ORG_USER.userId)) in branch (a); auth token is cleared or unchanged in branch (b)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] First POST /connect/token (grant_type=password) returns HTTP 200 — credentials valid
[API] Second POST /connect/token (grant_type=impersonate, user_id=@td(OTHER_ORG_USER.userId)) returns HTTP 401 or 403 — permission denied
[CONSOLE] No unhandled JS TypeError (a single info-level 'Failed to load resource ... /connect/token' line from the storefront propagating the 403 is acceptable and NOT a TypeError)
Failure_Signals: Both /connect/token calls return 200 (privilege escalation — impersonation succeeds without the permission); silent failure with NEITHER branch (a) NOR branch (b) firing (no alert, no /403 redirect — page hangs in indeterminate state); redirect into target session despite missing permission (JWT sub becomes @td(OTHER_ORG_USER.userId) — security regression); console TypeError
Cleanup: [NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}} to clear page state
References: VCST-4905, VCST-4725, BL-AUTH-005, BL-AUTH-006, BL-AUTH-009; RBAC gate negative test. UX implementation verified live 2026-05-12 — vcst-qa storefront does hard /403 redirect (branch b); branch (a) inline VcAlert kept in CSV for forward-compat if storefront rewrites the rejection UX; Re-verified PASS-CLEAN 2026-05-12 v2 via qa-frontend-expert — all 7 assertions matched, JWT sub stayed USR-007 (no privilege escalation), 200→403 token sequence confirmed (evidence: reports/test-lifecycle/IMP-036-reverify-20260512.md). Path to Automated: requires runner-native JWT-decode + sub-claim inequality assertion

Title: RBAC Propagation — Removing loginOnBehalf from Role Stops Subsequent Impersonations
Preconditions:
[PRE:CLEAR_SESSION]
Required state (independent of execution order): (1) Role '@td(AGENT_TEST_IMP_ROLE.name)' exists with platform:security:loginOnBehalf assigned. (2) Customer user '@td(AGENT_TEST_IMP_CUSTOMER.email)' has '@td(AGENT_TEST_IMP_ROLE.name)' assigned and a known storefront password. **Two-browser-context setup REQUIRED — do NOT share a single context:** STOREFRONT context = playwright-chrome (handles all `[STOREFRONT]`-prefixed steps: /sign-in + /account/impersonate). ADMIN context = playwright-edge (handles all `[ADMIN]`-prefixed steps: /workspace/security role mutations). Each context maintains its own cookies/session; an admin permission change in the ADMIN context must propagate via re-signin in the STOREFRONT context (fresh token claims) — verifying that propagation is the point of this test. State: assign permission → silent flow works → remove permission + fresh signin → silent flow no longer works.
Steps:
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/sign-in
[ACT] Fill 'Email': @td(AGENT_TEST_IMP_CUSTOMER.email)
[WAIT] Email field populated
[ACT] Fill 'Password': @td(AGENT_TEST_IMP_CUSTOMER.password)
[WAIT] Password field populated
[ACT] Click 'Sign In'
[WAIT] Authenticated session established (test operator identity)
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/account/impersonate/@td(IMPERSONATE_TARGET.userId)
[WAIT] Silent flow fires
[ASSERT] Impersonation banner visible (test operator → IMPERSONATE_TARGET)
[ACT] Click 'Stop impersonation' in banner / account menu
[WAIT] Operator session restored
[ADMIN] [ACT] Navigate to {{BACK_URL}}/#!/workspace/security > Roles > @td(AGENT_TEST_IMP_ROLE.name)
[ADMIN] [ACT] Open the role; in 'Assigned permissions' locate platform:security:loginOnBehalf
[ADMIN] [ACT] Select the permission row and click 'Remove'
[ADMIN] [ACT] Click 'Save' on the role
[ADMIN] [WAIT] Permission removed from role (Assigned permissions list no longer contains it)
[STOREFRONT] [ACT] Sign out and sign back in as @td(AGENT_TEST_IMP_CUSTOMER.email) (forces fresh token claims — new access_token issued without the impersonation claim)
[STOREFRONT] [WAIT] Authenticated session re-established; new token in storage (verify via DevTools Application > Local Storage if needed)
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/account/impersonate/@td(IMPERSONATE_TARGET.userId)
[WAIT] Response observed
[ASSERT] Silent flow does NOT fire — ImpersonateForm rendered or impersonation denied
[ASSERT] No new impersonation banner appears
Assertions:
[DOM] First impersonation attempt (with permission): banner visible — silent flow worked
[DOM] After permission removal + re-signin: ImpersonateForm IS rendered (silent path no longer engaged) OR a permission-denied page is shown
[STATE] Storefront session is NOT switched to IMPERSONATE_TARGET on the second attempt
[STATE] Permission removal propagated on next token issuance (no stale-claim impersonation)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] First /connect/token grant_type=impersonate returns HTTP 200 (with permission)
[API] Second /connect/token grant_type=impersonate (after removal + re-signin) returns HTTP 401/403 OR is not called at all (silent path gated)
[CONSOLE] No unhandled JS exceptions
Failure_Signals: Silent flow continues to fire despite permission removal; cached token still permits impersonation indefinitely after role change; console TypeError
Cleanup:
[ADMIN] [ACT] Open Roles > @td(AGENT_TEST_IMP_ROLE.name) > Assign > re-add platform:security:loginOnBehalf (optional — only if downstream tests depend on it)
[ADMIN] [ACT] Open Users > @td(AGENT_TEST_IMP_CUSTOMER.email) > Roles > Remove @td(AGENT_TEST_IMP_ROLE.name) > Save
[ADMIN] [ACT] Open Users > @td(AGENT_TEST_IMP_CUSTOMER.email) > Delete
[ACT] (Admin) Open Roles > @td(AGENT_TEST_IMP_ROLE.name) > Delete
[PRE:CLEAR_SESSION]
References: VCST-4905, BL-AUTH-005, BL-AUTH-006; RBAC propagation / claim refresh test

Title: Previous Impersonation Token Invalidated After Revert (AC#6 Security)
Preconditions:
[PRE:CLEAR_SESSION]
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:SUPPORT_AGENT]
SUPPORT_AGENT impersonates IMPERSONATE_TARGET via silent flow. The impersonation access_token is captured before revert. After revert, the captured token must be rejected (AC#6: 'Previous Token MUST be invalidated'). This test makes AC#6 falsifiable via a direct API call with the stale token.
Steps:
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/sign-in
[ACT] Fill 'Email': @td(SUPPORT_AGENT.email)
[WAIT] Email field populated
[ACT] Fill 'Password': @td(SUPPORT_AGENT.password)
[WAIT] Password field populated
[ACT] Click 'Sign In'
[WAIT] Authenticated session established
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/account/impersonate/@td(IMPERSONATE_TARGET.userId)
[WAIT] Impersonation active — banner visible; session is IMPERSONATE_TARGET
[ACT] Capture the current access_token from localStorage or cookie (via browser_evaluate: `window.localStorage.getItem('access_token')` or equivalent storefront token storage key) — store as {{IMPERSONATION_ACCESS_TOKEN}}
[ASSERT] {{IMPERSONATION_ACCESS_TOKEN}} is a non-empty JWT string
[ACT] Make a test API call using {{IMPERSONATION_ACCESS_TOKEN}} as Bearer token: GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/account — expect HTTP 200 (token is valid before revert)
[ASSERT] API call with {{IMPERSONATION_ACCESS_TOKEN}} returns HTTP 200 (token valid pre-revert)
[ACT] Click 'Revert back to own account' in header
[WAIT] Revert completes — operator session restored; banner gone
[ACT] Make the same API call again using {{IMPERSONATION_ACCESS_TOKEN}} as Bearer token: GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/account
[WAIT] Response
Assertions:
[STATE] Pre-revert: GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/account with {{IMPERSONATION_ACCESS_TOKEN}} returns HTTP 200 (token valid)
[STATE] Post-revert: GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/account with {{IMPERSONATION_ACCESS_TOKEN}} returns HTTP 401 (token invalidated — AC#6 satisfied)
[STATE] Operator session (current tab) is NOT affected by token invalidation — operator's own token is separate and still valid
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/account with stale {{IMPERSONATION_ACCESS_TOKEN}} returns HTTP 401 post-revert
[CONSOLE] No JS errors during revert
Failure_Signals: Post-revert API call with {{IMPERSONATION_ACCESS_TOKEN}} returns HTTP 200 (token still valid — AC#6 violation; security regression); token invalidation takes more than 5 seconds (delayed revocation window); operator's own session cleared by the invalidation call (incorrect scope of invalidation); console TypeError
References: VCST-4906, VCST-4725, BL-AUTH-007, BL-AUTH-011, ECL-14.1; Generated VCST-4906 / PR #2280 (2026-05-14); AC#6 explicit: Previous Token MUST be invalidated. PROPOSED BL: BL-AUTH-012 draft — 'Upon revert-impersonation, the impersonation access_token MUST be revoked on the server within the same request cycle; any API call using the revoked token MUST return HTTP 401.'

Title: Impersonation — Target User Account Status: Blocked and Invited Contracts
Preconditions:
[PRE:CLEAR_SESSION]
Operator (SUPPORT_AGENT) has StorefrontPermissions.CanImpersonate. Two separate target users required:
(a) IMPERSONATE_TARGET_BLOCKED: a customer account whose platform status is 'Locked' (user-level block, distinct from a suspended org). Platform Admin SPA shows Status=Locked on the user detail blade.
(b) IMPERSONATE_TARGET_INVITED: a customer account that was Invited (registered-email-sent) but has never completed email verification — platform status is typically 'Invited' or 'Pending'. Neither user has completed their first storefront sign-in.
NOTE: Both user fixtures require seeding before first run — see alias entries in test-data/aliases.json for provisioning instructions.
LOCKED CONTRACT (observed 2026-05-14, platform 3.1026.0, theme vc-theme-b2b-vue-2.49.0-pr-2280-8069-80690ef2):
(a) Blocked target: POST /connect/token grant_type=impersonate returns HTTP 200 with a valid Bearer token whose sub = blocked user. The storefront frontend detects the Locked status downstream, wipes its own auth state from localStorage, and redirects to /blocked (heading 'Your account is blocked'). NET RESULT: operator session is DESTROYED (auth.access_token cleared); no impersonation banner; navigating to /account redirects to /sign-in.
(b) Invited target: POST /connect/token grant_type=impersonate returns HTTP 200 with a valid Bearer token whose sub = invited user. Impersonation succeeds silently and lands on / (Home). Header shows the operator chip 'John Mitchell logged in as Invited User' + org chip 'AGENT-TEST-Org-TechFlow-20260310'. Account-menu popup exposes 'Back to John Mitchell' button. Revert restores operator session cleanly.
SECURITY: The platform OAuth endpoint has NO status-based gate. Direct REST callers with platform:security:loginOnBehalf can mint a usable 30-min Bearer for Blocked OR Invited targets, bypassing the storefront UI's /blocked redirect entirely. See tests/Sprint-current/VCST-4906/test-execution-report-imp-049.md for the full security analysis.
Steps:
--- PART A: Blocked User ---
[PRE:CLEAR_SESSION]
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/sign-in
[ACT] Fill 'Email': @td(SUPPORT_AGENT.email)
[WAIT] Email field populated
[ACT] Fill 'Password': @td(SUPPORT_AGENT.password)
[WAIT] Password field populated
[ACT] Click 'Sign In'
[WAIT] Authenticated session established
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/account/impersonate/@td(IMPERSONATE_TARGET_BLOCKED.userId)
[WAIT] Response observed — up to 10s for either silent flow completion OR a rejection signal
[ASSERT] LOCKED CONTRACT — Outcome (a-frontend-block) is observed: POST /connect/token grant_type=impersonate returns HTTP 200 with a valid Bearer token (sub=blocked_user_id, vc_operator_user_id=operator). The storefront detects the Locked status downstream, wipes auth from localStorage, and redirects to {{FRONT_URL}}/blocked (heading 'Your account is blocked'). No impersonation banner renders. The operator's own session is DESTROYED in the process — auth.access_token is cleared and /account redirects to /sign-in
[ASSERT] [API] POST /connect/token grant_type=impersonate returns HTTP 200 (NOT 4xx — platform has no status-based gate for Locked accounts; defense-in-depth is enforced only by the storefront UI's /blocked redirect)
[ASSERT] Final URL is /blocked (NOT /account/impersonate/{userId}, NOT /403, NOT a success redirect into target's account area)
[ASSERT] localStorage auth.access_token is null after the redirect — both the new (Blocked) and old (operator) tokens are cleared
[ASSERT] Operator session is NOT preserved: navigating to /account redirects to /sign-in?returnUrl=/account/dashboard. Operator must re-authenticate to continue work — this is a SIDE EFFECT of frontend-only Locked enforcement
--- PART B: Invited User ---
[PRE:CLEAR_SESSION]
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/sign-in
[ACT] Fill 'Email': @td(SUPPORT_AGENT.email)
[WAIT] Email field populated
[ACT] Fill 'Password': @td(SUPPORT_AGENT.password)
[WAIT] Password field populated
[ACT] Click 'Sign In'
[WAIT] Authenticated session established
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/account/impersonate/@td(IMPERSONATE_TARGET_INVITED.userId)
[WAIT] Response observed — up to 10s
[ASSERT] LOCKED CONTRACT — Outcome (b-success) is observed: POST /connect/token grant_type=impersonate returns HTTP 200 with a valid Bearer token (sub=invited_user_id, vc_operator_user_id=operator). Impersonation succeeds silently and final URL is {{FRONT_URL}}/ (Home). Storefront is fully functional under the Invited identity — catalog renders, account chrome shows the impersonated user
[ASSERT] [API] POST /connect/token grant_type=impersonate returns HTTP 200 (NOT 4xx — platform has no email-verification gate for impersonation; emailConfirmed=false does NOT block the impersonate grant)
[ASSERT] Header chrome shows the operator chip: 'John Mitchell logged in as Invited User' (operator-name-label span + 'logged in as' separator + target as Account menu trigger) + org chip 'AGENT-TEST-Org-TechFlow-20260310'
[ASSERT] Account-menu popup (click target name in header) exposes a 'Back to {operator_name}' revert button alongside the standard Logout
[ACT] Click 'Back to {operator_name}' in the account-menu popup
[ASSERT] After revert: operator session restored; localStorage auth.access_token decodes to sub=operator_user_id with vc_operator_user_id=null (no longer impersonating); landing URL is /company/members (operator's org home) or /account/dashboard
Assertions:
[STATE] Part A (Blocked) — LOCKED 2026-05-14: /connect/token grant_type=impersonate returns HTTP 200 with a valid Bearer token (issued for the Locked user). The storefront frontend wipes its own auth state and redirects to /blocked. NO impersonation banner. Operator session is DESTROYED as a side effect (auth.access_token cleared; navigating to /account redirects to /sign-in).
[STATE] Part A SECURITY GAP: defense-in-depth is enforced only by the storefront UI. Direct REST callers with platform:security:loginOnBehalf permission can bypass /blocked by calling /connect/token directly and using the issued token against /graphql or /xapi — see linked P1 bug.
[STATE] Part B (Invited) — LOCKED 2026-05-14: /connect/token grant_type=impersonate returns HTTP 200 with a valid Bearer token (issued for the Invited user). Impersonation succeeds silently and lands on /. Storefront is fully functional under the Invited identity. The operator chip + 'Back to {operator}' revert button render in the header. Revert restores the operator session cleanly.
[STATE] Part B SECURITY POSTURE: invited users who never verified email can be transparently signed-in-as by operators. emailConfirmed=false does NOT gate the impersonate grant. This may or may not be by design depending on the desired support workflow — see linked P2 question/bug.
[STATE] Part A side effect to flag: operator's session is NOT preserved across a denied/redirected Blocked impersonation attempt — this violates the previous expectation that 'operator's own session must remain intact (not cleared); no orphaned session left in localStorage' that was baked into the Negative_Cases column.
